CANCELLATION OF IDENTITY CARD (CNIC/CRC/NICOP/POC) Purpose:
Official process to deactivate an identity card when it is no longer valid, such as in cases of death, surrender of nationality, duplicate cards, or divorce (for foreign spouse). Scenarios:
1. Cancellation Due to Death: Immediate family members (parent, sibling, child, or spouse) of a deceased Pakistani citizen or exPakistani can apply for cancellation. Requires CNIC/NICOP of relative and death certificate from Union Council.
2. Surrender of Nationality: For citizens who have renounced Pakistani nationality after acquiring a foreign passport and are not allowed dual nationality.
3. Dual Nationality or Reacquired Pakistani Nationality: ExPakistanis who reacquire Pakistani nationality or whose country becomes eligible for dual nationality.
4. Divorce (Foreign Spouse Only): Pakistani spouse can apply for cancellation of exspouse’s POC after divorce.
5. Duplicate Cards (DUP Status): Citizens holding multiple ID cards must contact NADRA or email dup.op@nadra.gov.pk to clear duplicates. How to Apply at NADRA Registration Center (NRC):
 Visit any NADRA Registration Center.
 Receive a token.
 Photograph, fingerprints, and signature will be captured.
 Data will be entered and printed for review.
 Get the form attested by a blood relative or gazetted officer and submit back to NRC. Note:
“DUP” status means duplicate identity cards exist; applicant must request DUP clearance to retain the valid one. CHILD REGISTRATION CERTIFICATE (CRC / BFORM) Purpose:
Registers a child in NADRA’s database and establishes their parentage. It is a vital identity document for all children in Pakistan. Eligibility:
 Must provide proof of birth (Union Council birth certificate).
 At least one parent must have CNIC or NICOP.
 For children up to 1 year old, can apply online via Pak ID Mobile App.
 For children older than 1 year, application must be made at NADRA Registration Center (NRC).

Eligibility:
 Must provide proof of birth (Union Council birth certificate).
 At least one parent must have CNIC or NICOP.
 For children up to 1 year old, can apply online via Pak ID Mobile App.
 For children older than 1 year, application must be made at NADRA Registration Center (NRC). Applying via Pak ID Mobile App:
1. Log in or create a Pak ID account.
2. Select “Issuance of Identity Document” → “Child Registration Certificate (CRC)”.
3. Provide parent’s 13digit ID number and start the application.
4. Upload child’s photograph and required documents.
5. Provide digital signature and verify one parent’s fingerprints.
6. Review details and submit application. Applying via NADRA Registration Center (NRC):
1. Visit NRC and receive a token.
2. Photograph, fingerprints, and signature captured.
3. Review printed form and get it attested by gazetted officer (if spouse not present).
4. If both parents are present, attestation not required. Legal Note:
Under Section 9 of the NADRA Ordinance 2000, every child must be registered within one month of birth. Pak ID allows online CRC registration for children up to one year of age. FAMILY REGISTRATION CERTIFICATE (FRC) Purpose:
An official document verifying family composition as recorded in NADRA’s database. Commonly used for embassy and verification purposes but not a legal document. Categories:
1. By Birth: Shows parents and siblings.
2. By Marriage: Shows spouse and children.
3. By Adoption: Shows guardian and adopted children. Eligibility:
All family members must be registered with NADRA and possess valid 13digit identification numbers. Unregistered members will not appear in the FRC. Application Methods: At NADRA Registration Center (NRC):
 Receive token.
 Photographs of minors taken.
 Data entered and reviewed.
 Printed FRC handed over in real time.
 For children under 18, their presence is required.

At NADRA Registration Center (NRC):
 Receive token.
 Photographs of minors taken.
 Data entered and reviewed.
 Printed FRC handed over in real time.
 For children under 18, their presence is required. Through Pak ID Mobile App:
 Apply online through Pak ID platform.
 Upload data and verify details.
 FRC PDF sent via email within 24 hours to 2 working days. Documents Required:
 CNIC/CRC/NICOP/POC numbers of family members.
 Correct spellings as per NADRA record.
 Photographs of applicant and minors (<18 years).
 Unregistered family members must update CNIC before linking. Restrictions:
Only English name spellings can be corrected. Name changes of family members are not permitted within FRC. Delivery Time:
 Through NRC: Issued immediately.
 Through Pak ID: Delivered by email within 1–2 working days. JUVENILE CARD (JV) Purpose:
The Juvenile Card is a secure, chipbased identity document issued by NADRA to children under the age of 18. It serves as an official identity document, unlike the Child Registration Certificate (CRC), and grants access to multiple public services and facilities. Eligibility:
 Pakistani citizens under 18 years of age.
 Must have at least one parent or legal guardian with a valid CNIC or NICOP.
 Applicant must be physically present at the NADRA Registration Center (NRC). Requirements:
 Presence of a parent or blood relative (father, mother, brother, sister, daughter, or son) with a valid CNIC to provide biometrics.
 Original birth certificate or valid CRC of the applicant.
 Application must be submitted in person; no online service is available for Juvenile Cards.

Requirements:
 Presence of a parent or blood relative (father, mother, brother, sister, daughter, or son) with a valid CNIC to provide biometrics.
 Original birth certificate or valid CRC of the applicant.
 Application must be submitted in person; no online service is available for Juvenile Cards. How to Apply at NADRA Registration Center (NRC):
1. Visit any NADRA Registration Center.
2. Receive a token upon arrival.
3. Photograph, fingerprints, and signature of the child will be captured.
4. Required data will be entered and printed for review.
5. Review printed form for accuracy.
6. Get the form attested by a blood relative or gazetted officer.
7. Submit the attested form back to the NRC for processing. Processing & Delivery Time:
 Normal: 31 days
 Urgent: 15 days
 Fast Track: 9 days (priority queue and expedited processing) Note:
Processing time starts only after submission of the attested form. Online application via Pak ID Mobile App is currently not available for Juvenile Cards. SUCCESSION CERTIFICATE Purpose:
The Succession Certificate program enables legal heirs to obtain Letters of Administration or Succession Certificates through NADRA without having to approach the courts. This initiative streamlines inheritance matters for Pakistani citizens and significantly reduces the judicial burden. Background:
Launched in January 2021, the NADRA Succession Certificate system introduced a simplified, technologydriven process to issue inheritance certificates in a transparent and efficient manner. It has reduced the judiciary’s workload by nearly 30%. Key Features:
 Simplified Process: A fivestep process replaces lengthy court visits.
 Efficiency: Considerably faster than traditional court procedures.
 Transparency: Ensures secure verification and issuance.
 Convenience: Applicants can complete the process at NADRA centers instead of courts.
 Accessibility: Available at districtlevel NADRA offices in multiple provinces.

Key Features:
 Simplified Process: A fivestep process replaces lengthy court visits.
 Efficiency: Considerably faster than traditional court procedures.
 Transparency: Ensures secure verification and issuance.
 Convenience: Applicants can complete the process at NADRA centers instead of courts.
 Accessibility: Available at districtlevel NADRA offices in multiple provinces. Benefits:
 Eliminates the need for lengthy and costly court proceedings.
 Ensures transparent and reliable verification of legal heirs.
 Reduces overall time and effort for applicants.
 Provides official documentation recognized by relevant institutions. Regions Where Service Is Available:
 Islamabad Capital Territory (ICT)
 Punjab
 Sindh
 Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP) Note:
The Succession Certificate program will be expanded to additional regions across Pakistan. Applicants are advised to visit NADRA’s official website for the latest updates and availability. NATIONAL IDENTITY CARD (CNIC) Purpose:
The National Identity Card (CNIC) is the official identification document for Pakistani citizens aged 18 years and above. It establishes identity, citizenship, and eligibility for government services and benefits. NADRA issues, renews, modifies, and cancels CNICs under clearly defined processes. CNIC RENEWAL Purpose:
To renew an expired CNIC or one that is nearing expiry while maintaining the same personal information. Documents Required:
 Scanned image of existing CNIC or NICOP. Details:
 Only the photograph will be updated during renewal.
 If you wish to change any information (name, address, marital status, etc.), apply under the Modification category instead. CNIC MODIFICATION Purpose:
To update or correct information on an existing CNIC. Modification Types and Requirements: 1. Name Change  
    Name change not allowed in online applications.  
    Visit nearest NADRA Registration Center (NRC). 2. Parent Name  
    Not allowed in online applications.  
    Visit nearest NADRA Registration Center (NRC). 3. Date of Birth (D.O.B)  
    Cannot be modified online.  
    Visit NRC for manual correction.

2. Parent Name  
    Not allowed in online applications.  
    Visit nearest NADRA Registration Center (NRC). 3. Date of Birth (D.O.B)  
    Cannot be modified online.  
    Visit NRC for manual correction. 4. Present/Temporary Address Modification  
    No document required if adding a house and street number.  
    For unnumbered addresses, acceptable documents include:  
      Utility bill (electricity, gas, water)  
      Property document  
      Verified Local/Domicile/PRC Certificate  
      Affidavit or Rent Deed verified by house owner  
      Spouse’s or parent’s ID card with same address 5. Marital Status Modification  
    **Marriage:** Nikkahnama / Marriage Certificate / Affidavit attested by Oath Commissioner and spouse CNIC.  
    **Divorce:** Divorce Certificate from Union Council.  
    **Widow:** Death Certificate of spouse or attested affidavit.  
    **Unmarried to Widow/Divorce:** Marriage or Divorce Certificate / Court Decree / Affidavit.  
    Female applicants can only change status from *Married* to *Widowed*. 6. Religion Modification  
    **To Islam:** No document required. Reversion to Islam allowed anytime.  
    **From Islam to another religion:** Declaration/Undertaking form required.  
     (Modification not allowed if declaration of being Muslim was made after 13 Dec 2018.) 7. Place of Birth Modification  
    Requires documentary evidence for country change. 8. Mother Details  
    For firsttime addition, scanned copies of mother’s and sibling’s ID cards required. 9. Mother Tongue, Mark of ID, Qualification, Profession, Contact, and Relative Details  
    No document required unless requested by NADRA. 10. Disability Addition  
     Disability certificate from NCRDP/PCRDP or relevant social welfare authority required. CNIC CANCELLATION Purpose:
To formally cancel an existing CNIC in cases of death, nationality surrender, or dual nationality status. Types of Cancellation: 1. **Due to Death**  
    Immediate family member submits:  
      Cut (destroyed) CNIC of deceased  
      Death Certificate  
      Affidavit/Undertaking confirming death 2. **Due to Nationality Surrender**  
    Applicable when a citizen acquires a foreign passport and renounces Pakistani nationality.  
    Documents required:  
      Original CNIC/CRC/NICOP  
      Renunciation Certificate of Pakistani Nationality or valid foreign passport (nondual nationality country).

1. **Due to Death**  
    Immediate family member submits:  
      Cut (destroyed) CNIC of deceased  
      Death Certificate  
      Affidavit/Undertaking confirming death 2. **Due to Nationality Surrender**  
    Applicable when a citizen acquires a foreign passport and renounces Pakistani nationality.  
    Documents required:  
      Original CNIC/CRC/NICOP  
      Renunciation Certificate of Pakistani Nationality or valid foreign passport (nondual nationality country). 3. **Due to Dual Nationality or Reacquired Nationality**  
    Applicable for exPakistanis reacquiring Pakistani nationality or when a dualnationality agreement is announced with their current country. 4. **Duplicate ID Cards (DUP Status)**  
    Citizens with multiple CNICs must visit NADRA or email **dup.op@nadra.gov.pk** for resolution. EXPEDITED CNIC APPLICATION Purpose:
To upgrade an existing application from **Normal** or **Urgent** to **Executive** for faster processing. Steps to Expedite:
1. Log in to your Pak ID account.  
2. Accept terms and conditions and select the S/CNIC tab.  
3. Choose “I want to expedite my application.”  
4. Enter tracking ID and proceed.  
5. Pay the additional fee via credit card.  
6. Receive an autogenerated acknowledgment from NADRA. DUAL NATIONALITY COUNTRIES (Allowed by Law) 1. Australia – 15 Sep 1990

 Website: https://www.nadra.gov.pk PAKISTAN ORIGIN CARD (POC) Purpose:
The Pakistan Origin Card (POC) is an identification document issued by NADRA to Pakistaniorigin citizens living abroad. It establishes the holder’s Pakistani origin and enables access to certain rights and benefits. NEW POC APPLICATION Purpose:
To apply for a new Pakistan Origin Card based on one of four grounds: parents/grandparents, Pakistani spouse, exPakistani, or Foreigner of Pakistani Origin (FPO). Documents Required:
 Form 'Y' declaration in quadruplicate
 Original Renunciation Certificate
 Copies of CNIC/NICOP/POC
 Additional documents may be requested by the Case Officer Eligibility Scenarios: 1. Dual National Passport Holder:
 Cancel existing CNIC/NICOP before applying. 2. Parents/Grandparents Basis:
 Valid foreign passport of applicant
 CNIC/NICOP/POC/Pakistani Passport/MNIC/Domicile/Citizenship certificate of (grand)parents
 Birth Certificate or documents linking applicant to parents
 CNIC/NICOP of siblings/aunts/uncles/cousins with statement confirming relationship 3. Pakistani Spouse Basis:
 Valid foreign passport
 CNIC/NICOP/POC of Pakistani spouse
 Marriage Certificate and Affidavit from spouse
 For deceased spouse: Death Certificate and affidavit attested by Pakistan Mission 4. ExPakistani:
 Valid foreign passport
 CNIC/NICOP/Pakistani Passport/MNIC/Domicile/Citizenship Certificate/Renunciation Certificate
 Additional documents for those from East Pakistan pre16 Dec 1971 5. Foreigner of Pakistani Origin (FPO):
 Valid foreign passport
 Birth Certificate or document containing parent(s) names
 Affidavit/Death certificate of FPO with ID documents Dual National Countries (Partial List):
Australia, Belgium, Canada, Egypt, France, Iceland, Ireland, Italy, Jordan, Netherlands, New Zealand, Sweden, Switzerland, Syria, UK, USA, Bahrain, Finland, Denmark, Germany*, Norway
*Germany: Only children of Pakistani nationals born in Germany who acquired German nationality. POC EXPIRY / RENEWAL Purpose:
To renew an expired POC or one that is nearing expiry.

To formally cancel a POC in cases of nationality surrender or dual nationality arrangements. Documents Required:

 Original Renunciation Certificate
 Copies of CNIC/NICOP/POC

 Passport from dual national countries: Undertaking for POC Cancellation
 Surrendered nationality from other than dual national countries: Valid Foreign passport + Undertaking
 Ineligible if criteria not met EXPEDITING POC APPLICATION Purpose:
To upgrade an existing POC application from Normal/Urgent to Executive for faster processing. Steps to Expedite:

2. Accept terms and conditions and select the POC tab
3. Click “I want to expedite my application”
4. Enter tracking ID and press “Next”
5. Pay the additional fee via credit card
6. Receive an autogenerated acknowledgment from NADRA DUAL NATIONALITY COUNTRIES (Allowed by Law)

 Website: https://www.nadra.gov.pk NATIONAL IDENTITY CARD FOR OVERSEAS PAKISTANIS (NICOP/SNICOP) Purpose:
The NICOP/SNICOP is an official identity card issued by NADRA to Pakistani citizens living abroad. It establishes identity, nationality, and eligibility for government services and travel purposes. LOST/STOLEN NICOP Purpose:
To apply for a replacement of a stolen or lost NICOP. Documents Required:

Documents Required: 1. Applying from Single National Country (Nondual nationality countries):
 Pakistani Passport OR if not available, Undertaking from 2 witnesses 2. Applying from Dual National Countries:
 Foreign or Pakistani Passport OR if not available, Undertaking from 2 witnesses
 Note: For Germany, applicable only for children born from Pakistani parent and raised in Germany NICOP RENEWAL Purpose:
To renew an existing NICOP while keeping personal information unchanged. Documents Required:
 Form 'Y' declaration in quadruplicate
 Original Renunciation Certificate
 Scanned image of existing NICOP/CNIC
 Valid foreign or Pakistani passport (as per nationality rules)
 For single national countries, attested Undertaking from 2 witnesses if passport not available Notes:
 Only the photograph will be updated
 No document required for foreign address modification
 To modify other fields, apply under Modification category NICOP MODIFICATION Purpose:
To update or correct personal information on an existing NICOP. Modification Types and Requirements: 1. Date of Birth (D.O.B)
 Age change up to or more than 5 years: Valid foreign documents (Passport/Resident Permit/Work Permit/PR Card/Sojourn/Green Card)

Digital Identity Regulations S.R.O. 1956 of 2025 CNIC Services The Government of Pakistan, through the Ministry of Interior and the National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA), has issued the Digital Identity Regulations, 2025 under the powers conferred by Section 45 of the National Database and Registration Authority Ordinance, 2000. These regulations extend to the whole of Pakistan and come into force upon their publication in the Official Gazette. Chapter I – Preliminary
Short Title and Commencement These regulations may be called the NADRA Digital Identity Regulations, 2025. Definitions For the purposes of these regulations, unless the context otherwise requires: Authentication refers to the process of confirming an individual’s legal identity by matching credentials such as passwords, PINs, tokens, or biometric data against stored identity records. Authentication Service means a service provided by NADRA to verify identity data, demographic information, or biometric information of a Digital ID user and return a confirmation response or electronic Know Your Customer (e-KYC) data. Authority refers to the National Database and Registration Authority established under the NADRA Ordinance, 2000, which is responsible for issuing, renewing, regulating, and maintaining Digital Identity credentials. Alien Requesting Entity means any foreign individual, organization, or government agency that is not registered under the laws of Pakistan and seeks access to identity verification or authentication services. Biometric Data includes personal data resulting from technical processing of physical or behavioral characteristics such as fingerprints, facial recognition data, and iris scans. Card refers to the Computerized National Identity Card (CNIC) and National Identity Card for Overseas Pakistanis (NICOP), excluding Pakistan Origin Card and Alien Registration Card.

Biometric Data includes personal data resulting from technical processing of physical or behavioral characteristics such as fingerprints, facial recognition data, and iris scans. Card refers to the Computerized National Identity Card (CNIC) and National Identity Card for Overseas Pakistanis (NICOP), excluding Pakistan Origin Card and Alien Registration Card. Consent means a freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous indication by an individual, or their guardian, agreeing to the processing of personal data for a defined purpose. Broad or vague terms of use do not constitute valid consent. Citizen means a Pakistani national who has attained the age of eighteen years, including both resident and non-resident citizens. Data refers to any personal, non-personal, or aggregated information collected, processed, stored, or transmitted by NADRA, whether in electronic or physical form. Data Breach means unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, destruction, or loss of data. Digital Identity (Digital ID) refers to legally verifiable digital credentials issued by NADRA to eligible individuals with verified biometrics, enabling access to digital services and transactions. Digital Vault is a secure and encrypted repository under the control of the citizen for storing and sharing digital credentials and records. Electronic Know Your Customer (e-KYC) is an electronic verification service provided by NADRA using biometric data or one-time passwords to authenticate identity and issue verifiable credentials. National Data Exchange Layer (NDEL) is a secure digital framework that facilitates standardized data sharing among government entities while preserving data ownership and privacy. National Data Warehouse (NDW) refers to databases maintained by NADRA under Section 7 of the Ordinance. Requesting Entity means an agency that submits identity data for authentication or verification. Single Sign-On (SSO) allows a user to access multiple related systems after a single authentication, as defined under international standards.

Requesting Entity means an agency that submits identity data for authentication or verification. Single Sign-On (SSO) allows a user to access multiple related systems after a single authentication, as defined under international standards. User refers to a citizen who possesses and uses a Digital ID for identity verification and access to services. Chapter II – Regulation of Digital Identity
Issuance and Regulation NADRA shall regulate, maintain, and issue Digital IDs to eligible citizens possessing valid identity cards and verifiable biometrics upon request. Terms and Conditions A Digital ID may be suspended if the user no longer meets eligibility criteria or if the underlying identity card expires or is blocked. Reactivation is subject to renewal or unblocking of the card. User Onboarding Citizens must onboard through NADRA’s official mobile application by completing registration and verification steps. Identity verification may include personal details, biometric data, email address, and mobile number. Chapter III – Accessibility and Security
Accessibility for Persons with Disabilities NADRA shall ensure that Digital ID systems are accessible to persons with disabilities. Public and private entities integrating Digital ID services must provide reasonable accommodations and assistive support. Multilingual Accessibility Digital ID services shall be available in Urdu, English, and other regional languages as specified by the Constitution. Users shall be provided with a clear option to select their preferred language. Chapter IV – Consent and Data Protection
Consent Notice Before accessing personal data for authentication or verification, requesting entities must inform users about: The type and purpose of data being collected The legal grounds and duration of data processing Disclosure to third parties, if applicable Assurance that cross-border data transfer will not occur without explicit consent Chapter III – Consent Management and Data Security
Data Sharing

Disclosure to third parties, if applicable Assurance that cross-border data transfer will not occur without explicit consent Chapter III – Consent Management and Data Security
Data Sharing User data shall not be disclosed by any third party without the explicit written consent of the Authority. Data sharing must occur through secure and encrypted channels, ensuring confidentiality, integrity, and protection against unauthorised access, tampering, or cyber threats. Requesting entities must strictly comply with prohibitions related to biometric data as outlined in the Data Sharing Regulations. Data Security Requesting entities must ensure that all data centres, cloud storage facilities, servers, and information systems used for handling user data or authentication services are located within Pakistan. All NADRA-issued identifiers, including card numbers, must be stored exclusively within a designated encrypted vault. Such identifiers must not be replicated or stored in any other database. The vault must use strong encryption standards, role-based access controls, and maintain comprehensive audit logs in compliance with applicable laws. All onboarded requesting entities must comply with NADRA’s data security regulations, policies, standards, specifications, and guidelines issued from time to time. Chapter IV – Single Sign-On Mechanism
Scope and Eligibility NADRA shall provide Single Sign-On (SSO) authentication services to onboarded requesting entities to confirm that a user is a verified citizen. To be eligible for SSO: The user must complete onboarding through NADRA’s official mobile application. The user must possess a valid Digital ID issued after successful verification. User Consent for SSO When a user attempts to access an online service using SSO, they shall be prompted to provide explicit and informed consent authorising the sharing of limited profile data with the requesting entity. Ensure that fingerprints are clear and not smudged. If fingerprints are smudged, reprint the verifier form and apply fingerprints again. Media Brief Family Registration Certificate (FRC) and National Identity System Reforms The National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA), with approval from the Federal Cabinet, has introduced amendments to the National Identity Card Rules, 2002. These reforms aim to strengthen the national identity system by making it more secure, transparent, integrated, and resistant to fraud. These amendments apply nationwide to NADRA’s identity and family record systems. Since NADRA’s establishment in 2000, no major revisions had been made to the 2002 rules, making reforms necessary to meet current and future requirements. Reforms in Child Registration (B-Form) Under the revised rules, issuance of a child registration certificate (B-Form) without registration at the Union Council will no longer be permitted.

Reforms in Child Registration (B-Form) Under the revised rules, issuance of a child registration certificate (B-Form) without registration at the Union Council will no longer be permitted. Children up to three years of age are exempt from biometric data and photographs. For children aged three to ten years, a photograph is mandatory and iris scanning may be conducted where available. For children aged ten to eighteen years, both biometric data and photographs are mandatory, along with iris scanning where facilities exist. Each child will now be issued a separate B-Form with a mandatory expiry date. Fees have not been increased, and previously issued B-Forms remain valid. However, children applying for passports must obtain the new B-Form. These measures will improve child identification, increase transparency in registration, enhance birth registration at Union Councils, and prevent identity fraud and child trafficking. Reforms in Family Registration Certificate (FRC) The Family Registration Certificate has now been granted legal status. Applicants must submit a sworn declaration confirming the accuracy of family information provided. FRCs will be issued strictly based on NADRA’s existing records. Individuals cannot obtain an FRC by providing unverified or self-declared information. Family members such as parents, spouse, siblings, and children will be included in a single certificate. Citizens are required to ensure that unregistered family members are added to the NADRA database. Corrections to family records can be made through NADRA offices or online services. False information may result in legal action. These reforms will ensure accuracy of records and prevent misuse of family data. Identification Facilities for Married Women Married women are now allowed to choose whether their father’s name or husband’s name appears on their identity card. Seizure, Verification, Restoration, and Cancellation of Identity Documents

Married women are now allowed to choose whether their father’s name or husband’s name appears on their identity card. Seizure, Verification, Restoration, and Cancellation of Identity Documents Revised rules introduce a structured and lawful process for verification, seizure, suspension, and cancellation of identity documents to combat fraud. Verification boards at district, regional, and headquarters levels are required to decide cases within 30 days. Identity cards, B-Forms, and certificates may be seized or cancelled where necessary. These reforms ensure timely decisions and reduce misuse of identity documents. Non-Chip National Identity Card Improvements In addition to smart cards, NADRA issues a non-chip identity card with enhanced features. The card now includes bilingual information in Urdu and English, fingerprints, and a QR code instead of embedded biometrics. This card is cheaper than the smart card and is issued faster. Processing timelines are approximately 15 days for normal, 12 days for urgent, and 6 days for executive categories. This option improves accessibility for citizens unable to afford smart cards. Voluntary Disclosure of Incorrect Identity Information Citizens who have knowingly or unknowingly obtained identity documents using incorrect information are encouraged to report voluntarily to NADRA. Voluntary disclosure provides legal protection, while failure to report may result in legal action if fraud is discovered. Impounding refers to temporary suspension of an identity card or related data for investigation. Confiscation refers to permanent seizure of an identity card or data to prevent further use. Cancellation refers to permanent invalidation of identity documents. Minor citizens with disabilities will receive certificates or cards with prescribed validity periods. Citizens registered as organ donors may be issued identity cards displaying donor logos. Insufficient Data Security and Breach Notification Protocols There is no comprehensive framework specifically addressing data security measures for biometric data, nor are there mandatory breach notification protocols. No law explicitly governs biometric privacy. Impacts: Increased Vulnerability: Without stringent security controls, biometric data is more susceptible to breaches and unauthorized access. Delayed Response: The absence of mandatory breach notification mechanisms can delay incident response, worsening the impact of data breaches. Lack of Centralized Supervisory Authority There is no single, independent authority dedicated to overseeing biometric data protection. Regulatory oversight is fragmented across multiple bodies. Impacts: Inconsistent Enforcement: Fragmentation leads to uneven application of data protection standards.

Impacts: Inconsistent Enforcement: Fragmentation leads to uneven application of data protection standards. Coordination Challenges: Overlapping responsibilities cause delays, inefficiencies, and accountability gaps. Missing Biometrics in National Records NADRA began biometric enrollment in 2005. However: Approximately 1.2 million photographs and 2.7 million fingerprints are missing. Photograph acquisition for women was optional until 2011. There are significant biometric gaps for minors and non-nationals. Impacts: Security Risks: Inability to verify identities increases the risk of false or duplicate identities. Access to Services: Many services (banking, telecom, government assistance) rely on biometrics. Individuals without biometric records face exclusion. Human Trafficking Risks: Minors and women without biometric records are more vulnerable to exploitation. Confidentiality of Information Any person who unlawfully discloses information obtained through employment with NADRA is punishable by imprisonment up to five years, a fine up to one million rupees, or both. Disclosure is permitted only for criminal proceedings or when authorized by NADRA. Unauthorized use, access, or interference with NADRA’s registration systems, databases, or data infrastructure is punishable by rigorous imprisonment up to fourteen years and a minimum fine of one million rupees. Offences and Penalties Offences include failure to register, concealment of information, failure to report lost cards, refusal to surrender identity documents, late application for CNIC, or violation of NADRA rules. Penalties include imprisonment up to six months or fines. Serious offences such as false statements, tampering with identity cards, forging documents, possessing multiple cards unlawfully, or fraudulent attestation are punishable by imprisonment up to five years and fines up to one hundred thousand rupees. All fines collected are credited to the Federal Consolidated Fund. Legal Proceedings Courts may only take cognizance of offences upon a written complaint by NADRA or an authorized officer. Governance and Employment The Chairman and members are paid emoluments determined by the Federal Government and may not engage in outside employment, business, or advisory roles related to NADRA projects. They must not hold financial interests in entities dealing with NADRA. NADRA may appoint registration officers, staff, consultants, and advisers as required. All such personnel are deemed public servants under Pakistani law. Transfer of Employees and Assets Upon NADRA’s establishment, employees of predecessor organizations were transferred to NADRA with preserved service conditions. Assets, liabilities, contracts, and legal proceedings were also transferred to NADRA. Employees could opt out of transfer within specified periods. No compensation is payable solely due to transfer. Tax Exemption NADRA is exempt from income tax, super tax, and wealth tax for five years from establishment. Rule-Making Authority The Federal Government may issue rules, and NADRA may issue regulations, to implement the Ordinance. NADRA regulations override other laws where conflicts exist. Applicants select a processing category: Normal Urgent Executive The delivery method for the renewed card must be selected. Disability details, if applicable, may be added by entering the disability type, certificate number, and issuance date. This section is optional.
